sessions.title: Registration and Checkin open all day!
sessions.description: Come get your swag, grab a quick snack, and get ready to start the best day of your week!

sessions.title: The Foundation Powering Creative Pipelines
sessions.description:
When CentOS went away, the VFX world lost the dependable floor it had been standing on for a decade. The industry has spent the years since settling on what comes next.

For a huge part of it, that answer is AlmaLinux. Over the last year, the VFX industry has helped mold AlmaLinux into what it needs in a variety of ways. With native NVIDIA support, Btrfs, broader hardware support, easier migrations, and more than two million systems now checking in for updates every week (roughly double where we were a year ago), the VFX industry is finding what it needs in the AlmaLinux Community. All of it forever-free and owned by the community that uses it.

This talk opens the day with a look at where AlmaLinux is, how we got here, and what we're powering next.

sessions.title: Fueling the Frame: What’s New with the AlmaLinux Media & Entertainment SIG
sessions.description:
Born out of direct community discussions at SIGGRAPH 2024 and 2025, the AlmaLinux Media & Entertainment (M&E) Special Interest Group (SIG) was spun off with a clear mission: to address the unique, high-performance demands of creative pipelines, from heavy render nodes to artist workstations.

A lot has happened since our initial foundations. In this session, we are diving straight into the concrete tools, testing, and future-proofing we’ve been building over the last year.

We will explore our recent engineering efforts, including comprehensive testing against AlmaLinux 10 with KDE Plasma and tackling the industry's next major operational frontiers: Wayland support and remote desktop solutions. Furthermore, we’ll showcase how we are actively lowering the barrier to entry with a brand-new, Qt-based GUI application designed to seamlessly kickstart freelancers and students into the Linux VFX ecosystem. Join us to see how the M&E SIG is translating SIGGRAPH community feedback into production-ready open-source infrastructure.

What Attendees Will Learn (Key Takeaways)

Origins & Purpose: Why the M&E SIG was spun off following SIGGRAPH 2024 and 2025 to bridge the gap between enterprise Linux and the rigid demands of studio pipelines.

The Tech Stack of Tomorrow: An inside look at our testing and development tracking toward AlmaLinux 10, KDE Plasma, and solving the Wayland + remote desktop equation for color-accurate, low-latency artist workflows.

Lowering the Friction: A showcase of our new Qt-based desktop GUI built specifically to onboard students and freelance artists onto Linux without the terminal dread.

The Roadmap & Collaboration: How studios can leverage these community-driven tools today, and how to get involved in the SIG's future initiatives.

sessions.title: Introducing : AlmaLinux M&E SIG Creative Installer
sessions.description: Introducing the AlmaLinux Creative Installer, an open source tool from the AlmaLinux Media & Entertainment SIG that bootstraps a creative workstation with curated tools and desktop tweaks. Bring questions on usage, contributing, the Qt migration, packaging/COPR, or supported OS/DE combos.

sessions.title: Break!
sessions.description: Stretch your legs, refill your water, and say hi to your neighbor

sessions.title: Does your EDR actually do that?
sessions.description: Just because a product says it supports Linux doesn’t mean you get all the features they brag about. Many of those are only on Windows. So let’s talk about what your EDR can and can’t do on your Linux workstations.

sessions.title: 5 Practical Tips to Improve your Linux log file Analysis Efficiently
sessions.description:
Linux now stands as the most widely used operating system for production and development environments, as it provides a lot of services (daemons) and tools for debugging.

One core part are the log files which is widely used. You can use built-in commands such as less, grep, awk/sed or tail for log analysis, but there are times when you need some more advanced commands or tools that can help to investigate large log files efficiently.

In this talk, you will learn five practical tips to view your log files for analysis. You will learn about Open Source tools that can make your job easier for your daily tasks.

Join us to learn more about Linux, log analysis, aggregation tools, and the value of Open Source Community.

sessions.title: Lunch!
sessions.description: Lunch will be provided onsite, with vegetarian and gluten free options available.

sessions.title: Introducing the Wayland for Artists ASWF Working Group
sessions.description:
This session introduces the Academy Software Foundation's recently launched Wayland for Artists Working Group. 

The graphical desktop on Linux workstations in animation and VFX facilities has long relied on the X Windows System (X11) protocol. Wayland is a long running effort to replace X11 with a more modern protocol architecture. The Xorg server and other X servers (except Xwayland) have been removed from Enterprise Linux 10 and subsequent releases. 

The full impact of this transition on high-end content creation applications (commercial, open source and in-house) and workflows used in animation and VFX as well as remote access solutions is still not well understood in our industry.

This new Working Group now provides a forum to convene the community to ensure the long-term viability of Linux as the premier platform for professional VFX and animation artists.

sessions.title: Workshop! AlmaLinux M&E For You
sessions.description:
The AlmaLinux M&E edition provides an integrated experience leveraging AlmaLinux OS 10 with KDE Plasma along with a selection of open source media creation tools. But for studios looking to leverage AlmaLinux M&E in their environments, producing tailored experiences matching their software and hardware requirements is a must.

This workshop aims to showcase how to derive from the official deliverable to produce tailored versions optimized for your requirements to make mass deployment as smooth as possible.

sessions.title: Break!
sessions.description: We're almost done! Take a few minutes to refill your beverage of choice, and get ready for the rest of the afternoon.

sessions.title: The Next Display Layer: A Live Preview of Amazon DCV's Future on Linux
sessions.description:
The way we build Linux workstations is fundamentally evolving, and the industry's reliance on legacy display layers is drawing to a close. As studios transition toward a modern desktop future, infrastructure teams face a singular challenge: maintaining the high-performance, enterprise-grade remote protocols that our pipelines demand. 

In this technical demonstration, engineered in collaboration with the remote desktop experts at NI SP GmbH, we are unveiling a critical piece of that puzzle: a live preview of the Amazon DCV beta running on the next generation of the Linux desktop stack. No fluff, no deep-dive architecture theory, just a direct look at the low-latency, high-fidelity future of remote creative workflows. 

If you are mapping your studio's hardware and protocol lifecycle, this is the session you can't afford to miss.

sessions.title: You don't have to be a Jedi
sessions.description:
You have spent the day hearing what the AlmaLinux Media and Entertainment community is building, from the new M&E SIG and the move to AlmaLinux 10 to Wayland for artists, remote desktop, and studio-ready images. This closing session is about you. Whether you run a render farm, light shots, wrangle a pipeline, coordinate a crew, or write the docs nobody else volunteers for, there is a real role for you in this project, and almost none of them require you to be a kernel wizard. 

Today's sessions were really a practical map of how to participate in the AlmaLinux project. Let benny show you at least one thing you can do before you leave the room. You don't even have to be a Jedi.

speakers.bio:
benny is the Chair of the AlmaLinux OS Foundation, with a long history in business and community building and an undying love of open source. 

I spell my name with a lowercase b, but sessionize doesn't support that...yet.
speakers.tagLine: Chair, Board of Directors @ AlmaLinux OS Foundation

speakers.bio: Chris Taylor is a security consultant with a background in digital forensics and incident response who specializes in building security programs for growing companies.

Partner Solutions Architect. David has been working on Linux solutions at Amazon Web Services since 2012.  David has more than 16 years experience deploying and developing open source solutions for customers as a consultant and a systems designer. David was elected to the CentOS Board in 2015 and participates as a member of the Fedora Project.
DCV Remote Desktops, High End Remote Streaming
speakers.tagLine: NI SP GmbH - DCV Remote Desktops / CEO

speakers.bio: Neal Gompa is a developer for – and contributor to – Fedora, AlmaLinux, CentOS, and openSUSE. Neal focuses primarily on the base Linux system components, such as package and software management, and desktop Linux. He believes in “upstream first,” which has led him all over the open source world. In addition to open source work as a consultant through Velocity Limitless, he is also a co-host on the Sudo Show podcast where he talks about "the business of Linux."

Nick is the Chair of the Visual Effects Technology Committee, co-founder of the VFX Reference Platform, and a member of the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences. Nick started his career in film over two decades ago as a Linux Systems Administrator. With experience spanning animation and visual effects on more than 100 films, he now serves as SVP of Production and Technology at Walt Disney Animation Studios.

Usman is a Staff Developer Advocate at Grafana Labs from Nuremberg, Germany. He works with the Open Source community on the community forum, GitHub and Slack.

He has over 15 years of experience in IT and in Cloud Stack where he served multiple customers all over Europe, US, Japan, etc.

He is an active international public speaker participating in multiple conferences and events.

In his free time, Usman likes to spend time with his family, go out on occasional traveling and play games or read comics.
speakers.tagLine: Staff Developer Advocate at Grafana Labs

speakers.bio: Infrastructure & Systems Security Expert | Lead, AlmaLinux M&E SIG, build and secure the digital foundations that allow companies to transform technical complexity into operational stability. My philosophy is rooted in pragmatic security: infrastructure should be invisible, highly available, and community-validated to truly drive business objectives.
